reason: Verify MCP failures are structured, actionable, and not success-shaped.
Verify protocol output stays clean and operational logs use stderr.
Verify TypeScript quality, lint, build, and backend test expectations.
Verify carried-forward safety constraints for paths, type names, resources, and commands.
Verify plugin lifecycle setup/teardown is explicit and safe.
Verify plugin-side contract validation and editor object safety.
Verify plugin scaffold and service boundary quality requirements.
Check for duplicated constants, validation logic, or tool response shapes.
Check consistency across MCP server, editor transport, and plugin payload contracts.
